channel.title: Serhat Demirkol | EHS Product & Systems & Data
channel.description: Insights on EHS systems design, data architecture, and operational risk management.

item.title: AI Generates Clues. Investigators Write Findings.
item.description: The investigation workflow that keeps AI in its place: where it assists, where it is prohibited, and the evidence requirement that closes the bypass.

item.title: Redirecting...
Safety Form Usability: The Friction Budget Method
item.description: Why safety forms fail in the field and how to use a quantitative Friction Budget to design high-integrity safety applications that frontline workers won't bypass.

item.title: AI Constructs Root Causes. It Cannot Identify Them.
item.description: AI root cause analysis is a hypothesis, not a finding. Three structural limits explain why — and three tests to run on any vendor that claims otherwise.

item.title: Why Frontline Workers Fabricate Safety Data
item.description: Why frontline workers enter fabricated safety data into EHS apps and the five technical countermeasures that verify site presence and resolve reporting silos.

item.title: Your Safety Timestamps Are Testimony, Not Fact
item.description: A safety record's timestamp is testimony, not proof. Offline sync lets device clocks lie. Dual timestamps prevent a device clock from passing as evidence.

item.title: 2026 EHS Tech Consensus: Interrogating the Vendor AI Data
item.description: 97% claim AI adoption. 5% have it embedded. 85% still rely on manual tools. What the 2026 EHS vendor surveys reveal when you read past the headline.

item.title: The Digital Error Trap: Interface Risk and EHS Data Integrity
item.description: Digital error traps corrupt the data safety leadership uses to set priorities. What they are, how they propagate through the record, and what to act on.

item.title: The EHS AI Readiness Checklist
item.description: Answer the following questions to receive your personalized AI Readiness score and a tailored action plan.

item.title: Validating Leading Indicators: Auditing EHS Data for Predictive Signal
item.description: Most EHS programmes track leading indicators by convention, not validation. Applies lag-correlation analysis to a 36-month dataset.

item.title: Structuring Unstructured EHS Data with Targeted Extraction
item.description: Incident narratives, audit notes, near-miss reports — none of it is machine-readable. How targeted extraction frameworks turn unstructured text into data.

item.title: The "Feature Trap" in EHS Software Selection
item.description: Choosing EHS software often feels like a checklist exercise. Procurement teams and safety leaders spend months documenting hundreds of required features.

item.title: The EHS AI Launchpad: Introducing Intelligence Incrementally
item.description: Building intelligence one step at a time. Learn how to deploy your first high-value AI tool without getting stuck in pilot purgatory.

item.title: The EHS Data Refinery: Building AI-Ready Safety Data
item.description: Learn how to build an EHS Data Refinery to transform crude, unstructured data into AI-ready intelligence through governance, cleansing, and labeling strategies.

item.title: EHS AI Interoperability: Building the Foundation
item.description: Close the AI Readiness Gap in EHS through interoperability. Learn how connecting EHS, HR, and Maintenance data unlocks powerful predictive safety analytics.

item.title: The AI Readiness Gap in EHS
item.description: Close the AI Readiness Gap in EHS. Learn why treating AI as a software feature fails and how a phased data refinery leads to successful deployment.

item.title: The Requirements Iceberg in EHS Software
item.description: Every EHS software request that looks simple conceals an iceberg of complexity. Learn why a detailed blueprint is required for reliable system configuration.

item.title: Measuring Success: Real-World ROI of EHS Software
item.description: EHS software ROI is often just post-rationalisation. Learn how to measure real-world impact by focusing on information efficiency and field reality.

item.title: EHS for Enterprises: Strategies for Global Success
item.description: Learn how to implement EHS software in large enterprises, addressing scalability, ERP integration, and the balance between global standards and local relevancy.

item.title: Strategic EHS Software Implementation
item.description: Deploy EHS software successfully with a strategic, phased approach. Learn about needs analysis, vendor selection, change management, and post-launch monitoring.

item.title: EHS Software: More Than a Technological Fix
item.description: Why EHS software is more than a plug-and-play solution. The strategic approach needed for successful implementation and the common pitfalls to avoid.

item.title: Human Element in EHS Software Success
item.description: Unlock EHS software success by focusing on the human element. Learn how culture, leadership, and user buy-in are critical for maximizing technology investments.

item.title: Diagnostic Analytics: Enhancing Workplace Safety
item.description: Accurate incident recording is only half the battle. Use diagnostic analytics to uncover systemic root causes and move from reactive to proactive safety.

item.title: ESAW Methodology Guide: Accident Recording
item.description: Accurate accident recording is the foundational architecture of an OHS system. Learn how the ESAW methodology transforms reporting into a diagnostic tool.

item.title: Getting Trapped While Controlling Hazards
item.description: Hazard controls introduce their own failure modes. Error traps — systemic conditions that make mistakes more likely.

item.title: Why EHS Software is Infrastructure, Not a Technological Fix
item.description: EHS software fails as a compliance tool. It succeeds as data architecture for operational truth. Learn why infrastructure beats technological fixes for EHS.

item.title: Analysis and Planning for EHS Software Implementation
item.description: Implementations are won or lost in the planning phase. Learn how to map operational reality and build a project plan that avoids common EHS deployment pitfalls.

item.title: How Machine Learning Can Improve Workplace Incident Classification
item.description: How machine learning automates workplace incident classification — the benefits for EHS professionals and a practical tool in action.
